response:
The `/decide` process is complete — weighed by an advisor panel, red-teamed, and recorded. The ADR is saved at `docs/decisions/0001-free-tier-daily-cap.md`. Here's the decision record:

---

# ADR 0001 — Free-tier daily request cap at billing launch

**Status:** accepted · **Date:** 2026-07-12 · **Decider:** PM (accountable) · **Consulted:** advisor panel (low / high / reversibility), red-team · **Supersedes:** MEMO.md per-key framing; refined Option C

## Context
Turning on billing: free tier + $12/mo Pro (unmetered, fair-use). The free cap is the last open item. 30 days of logs show ~40 active keys/day, ~100 req/active-key/day (verified range 93-108). "Busiest keys under ~250/day" is a **founder anecdote** — the CSV is daily aggregate only, so no per-key percentile is derivable; it is demoted below. No conversion data exists (Pro not live), so any cap's upgrade effect is a guess. The metering rig (per-key Redis counter, 24h window, alerts, dashboard) is built, load-tested to 1,000/day, and already holds real per-key counts.

## Decision
**Launch a single flat free cap of 500 req/24h, enforced per verified account.** The conversion lever is deferred, not chosen.
1. **500/day**, provisional on one cheap pre-launch check. If measured per-key p99 > 250, set the cap to `max(500, 2 × p99)` before flipping.
2. **Per verified account.** All of an account's free keys share one budget; issuance gated to a verified account — otherwise the cap dies to minting key #2.
3. **Flat, not cohort-split** — same cap for all; no grandfathering machinery, no "new users get a worse deal" optics. 500 is ~2× stated busiest usage, so no current user (incl. a single CI burst) is throttled at launch.
4. **Good-faith caps move up only, never down.** ToS/abuse enforcement is carved OUT of this promise.
5. **Lever deferred.** A lower cap for NEW accounts may come later, once Pro data exists — an up-safe move for existing accounts.

## Binding precondition (launch-blocking)
Free-key issuance must be gated to one verified account with the cap enforced at account granularity before billing flips. An ungated per-key free tier meters nothing and converts no one. `[checkable]` against auth/key-issuance code (not in this repo; founder confirms).

## Rationale
Under two stacked unknowns — real per-key distribution AND conversion elasticity — a "lever" number (150/200) fakes precision we lack; you cannot tune a lever with zero conversion data. At ~40 users the scarce resource is adoption/word-of-mouth, and throttling engaged users the same day billing arrives is the one move you cannot take back. 500 protects every current user yet stays a real boundary: sustained heavy use (>15k/mo) crosses it and $12 Pro is the fair answer. Sequence: launch generous, buy the data, then design the lever for the NEW cohort. Per-account enforcement makes the minting exploit impossible, not merely detectable.

## Alternatives rejected
- **A, 1,000/day** — differs from 500 only in unused headroom nobody reaches; picked "day one, felt generous," not derived from demand.
- **B, 150/day lever** — tuned to a conversion effect we have no data for; clips highest-intent users and CI bursts before they see the Pro price; rides on the unverifiable ~250 figure.
- **Grandfather existing at 1,000 + cap new at 150-200 (panel reframe)** — rejected after red-team: rides on ~250, permanently exempts (up-only-forever) the 40 proven-engaged accounts most likely to pay, adds fragile `created_at` machinery, and creates "old users get a better free tier" optics. Segmentation kept but deferred until there is data.

## Assumptions
- `[judgment]` Early supporters convert better via Pro's positive value than via a takeaway; if Pro converts NONE of the 40 in 90 days, that flags a weak Pro value prop — a bigger finding than cap-tuning.
- `[judgment]` 500/day preserves goodwill yet still binds genuinely heavy users.
- `[checkable]` The rig's per-key counters yield p50/p95/p99 in an afternoon — the cheap check replacing the ~250 anecdote (founder runs before flip).
- `[checkable]` Issuance can be gated per verified account (binding precondition).

## Revisit triggers
- Pre-launch p99 > 250 → launch cap = `max(500, 2 × p99)`.
- After 60-90 days of Pro data: a distinct heavy segment near 500 AND weak Pro conversion → add a lower binding cap for NEW accounts only (the deferred lever).
- >25-30% of accounts hit 500/day → raise it unless it drives healthy conversion.
- Abuse erodes margin → ToS/abuse path (carved out of up-only), not a good-faith cut.

## Red-team responses (folded in)
- Key-minting defeats per-key cap `[fatal]` → per-account enforcement + abuse carve-out.
- ~250 unverifiable `[fatal]` → demoted; cheap p99 pull made binding; number provisional.
- Grandfather-forever exempts best cohort `[fatal]` → flat cap; lever deferred with a data trigger instead of frozen into policy.
- Burst clipping / simpler flat cap / fragile `created_at` `[fix-and-keep]` → 500 absorbs CI bursts; flat adopted; machinery removed.
- Launch comms `[minor]` → message the new dashboard/alerts as "new visibility, nothing taken away," separately from billing.

---

**Two things worth your attention as founder:** the binding precondition (account-gated key issuance) needs confirming against your actual auth code before you flip billing — the whole decision rests on it. And the deferred-lever bet (early supporters convert via Pro's value, not via a throttle) is the assumption most worth watching once Pro data starts coming in.

# ADR 0001 — Free-tier daily request cap at billing launch

## Context

Turning on billing: free tier + $12/mo Pro (unmetered, fair-use). The free cap is
the last open item. Evidence: 30 days of logs show ~40 active keys/day, ~100
req/active-key/day (verified range 93-108). "Busiest keys under ~250/day" is a
founder anecdote — the CSV is daily aggregate only, so no per-key percentile is
derivable; it is demoted below. No conversion data exists (Pro not live), so any
cap's upgrade effect is a guess. The metering rig (per-key Redis counter, 24h
window, alerts, dashboard) is built, load-tested to 1,000/day, and already holds
real per-key counts.

## Decision

**Launch a single flat free cap of 500 req/24h, enforced per verified account.**
The conversion lever is deferred, not chosen.

1. **500/day**, provisional on one cheap pre-launch check. If measured per-key p99
   exceeds 250, set the cap to `max(500, 2 x p99)` before flipping.
2. **Per verified account.** All of an account's free keys share one budget;
   free-key issuance is gated to a verified account. Otherwise the cap dies to
   minting key #2.
3. **Flat, not cohort-split** — same cap for all, no grandfathering machinery, no
   "new users get a worse deal" optics. 500 is ~2x stated busiest usage, so no
   current user (incl. a single CI burst) is throttled at launch.
4. **Good-faith caps move up only, never down.** ToS/abuse enforcement (key farming,
   resale, botting) is carved OUT of this promise.
5. **Lever deferred.** A lower cap for NEW accounts may come later, once Pro
   conversion data exists — an up-safe move for existing accounts.

## Binding precondition (launch-blocking)

Free-key issuance must be gated to one verified account with the cap enforced at
account granularity before billing flips. An ungated per-key free tier meters
nothing and converts no one. [checkable] against auth/key-issuance code (not in this
repo; founder confirms).

## Rationale

Under two stacked unknowns — real per-key distribution AND conversion elasticity — a
"lever" number (150/200) fakes precision we lack; you cannot tune a lever with zero
conversion data. At ~40 users the scarce resource is adoption/word-of-mouth, and
throttling engaged users the same day billing arrives is the one move you cannot take
back. 500 protects every current user yet stays a real boundary: sustained heavy use
(>15k/mo) crosses it and $12 Pro is the fair answer. Sequence: launch generous, buy
the data, then design the lever for the NEW cohort (up-safe, costs no trust).
Per-account enforcement makes the minting exploit impossible, not merely detectable.

## Alternatives rejected

- **A, 1,000/day** — differs from 500 only in unused headroom nobody reaches; picked
  "day one, felt generous," not derived from demand.
- **B, 150/day lever** — tuned to a conversion effect we have no data for; clips
  highest-intent users and CI bursts before they see the Pro price; rides on the
  unverifiable ~250 figure.
- **Grandfather existing at 1,000 + cap new at 150-200 (panel reframe)** — rejected
  after red-team: rides on ~250, permanently exempts (up-only-forever) the 40
  proven-engaged accounts most likely to pay, adds fragile `created_at` machinery,
  and creates "old users get a better free tier" optics. Segmentation is kept but
  deferred until there is data.

## Assumptions

- [judgment] Early supporters convert better via Pro's positive value than via a
  takeaway; if Pro converts NONE of the 40 in 90 days, that flags a weak Pro value
  prop — a bigger finding than cap-tuning.
- [judgment] 500/day preserves goodwill yet still binds genuinely heavy users.
- [checkable] The rig's per-key counters yield p50/p95/p99 in an afternoon — the
  cheap check replacing the ~250 anecdote (founder runs before flip).
- [checkable] Issuance can be gated per verified account (binding precondition).

## Revisit triggers

- Pre-launch p99 > 250 -> launch cap = `max(500, 2 x p99)`.
- After 60-90 days of Pro data: a distinct heavy segment near 500 AND weak Pro
  conversion -> add a lower binding cap for NEW accounts only (the deferred lever).
- >25-30% of accounts hit 500/day -> raise it unless it drives healthy conversion.
- Abuse erodes margin -> ToS/abuse path (carved out of up-only), not a good-faith cut.

## Red-team responses (folded in)

- Key-minting defeats per-key cap [fatal] -> per-account enforcement + abuse carve-out.
- ~250 unverifiable [fatal] -> demoted; cheap p99 pull made binding; number provisional.
- Grandfather-forever exempts best cohort [fatal] -> flat cap; lever deferred with a
  data trigger instead of frozen into policy.
- Burst clipping / simpler flat cap / fragile `created_at` [fix-and-keep] -> 500
  absorbs CI bursts; flat adopted; created_at machinery removed.
- Launch comms [minor] -> message the new dashboard/alerts as "new visibility, nothing
  taken away," separately from billing.
